description_long: This object is a traditional stringed musical instrument with a long, rectangular wooden body and a series of raised bridges supporting multiple strings stretched across its length. The body is a rich brown wood, with lighter wood used for the bridges and end pieces. At one end, there are blue and red decorative elements, possibly for tuning or string anchoring, adding a touch of color and visual interest. The strings are evenly spaced and run parallel to each other, with the bridges arranged in a diagonal pattern. The surface is smooth and polished, reflecting the craftsmanship typical of East Asian zithers such as the koto or guzheng. The instrument is designed to be played while resting on a flat surface, with the player plucking or striking the strings.
description: A long, rectangular wooden stringed instrument with multiple bridges and colorful end decorations. The polished wood body and diagonal bridge arrangement give it a refined, traditional appearance.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden body, the parallel strings, and the diagonal arrangement of the bridges.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the blue and red decorative elements and the arrangement of the strings and bridges.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the length of the instrument, the strings, and the bridges from a different angle.
description_view_3: View from the other end, focusing on the plain wooden end and the diagonal line of bridges.
